attraction_name: Nanjing City Wall Taicheng Scenic Area
description: The best-preserved section of the Ming Dynasty city wall, offering panoramic views of Xuanwu Lake.

attraction_name: Purple Mountain Observatory, Chinese Academy of Sciences
description: The birthplace of modern Chinese astronomy, providing a panoramic view of Nanjing, and a science education base.
China's first national institution of higher learning, a core scenic area of the Qinhuai River landscape, with well-preserved ancient architecture.
A famous ancient temple in Nanjing, now a national revolutionary martyrs' cemetery, featuring a serene environment and the iconic Linggu Pagoda.
The mausoleum of Dr. Sun Yat-sen, a memorial site for the founding father of the Republic of China, and a core attraction of the Zhongshan Scenic Area.
The residence of Chiang Kai-shek and Soong Mei-ling, featuring French architectural style, known as the 'First Villa of the Far East.'
China's largest imperial garden lake, praised as the 'Pearl of Jinling,' offering diverse scenery across all seasons.
A preserved district of Ming and Qing Dynasty architecture, blending traditional residences with modern commerce, providing an experience of old Nanjing's charm.
A sacred site commemorating China's New Democratic Revolution, a solemn place to honor revolutionary martyrs.
The tomb of Zhu Yuanzhang, the founding emperor of the Ming Dynasty, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a model of Ming and Qing imperial mausoleums.
A famous snack street in Nanjing, gathering authentic local delicacies and a popular dining destination recommended by locals.
One of China's three major museums, featuring extensive collections and six exhibition halls, including history, art, and the Republic of China galleries.
A renowned food street in Nanjing, offering a variety of specialty snacks and night market delicacies, showcasing authentic Nanjing flavors.

attraction_name: Music Stage at Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum Scenic Area, Zhongshan Scenic Area
description: An open-air music stage built during the Republic of China era, known for its exquisite architecture and elegant, tranquil surroundings.
The site of an ancient Southern Dynasty temple, now a cemetery for fallen soldiers of the National Revolutionary Army, with the iconic Linggu Pagoda as its landmark.
The mother river of Nanjing, where night cruises offer views of ancient architectural clusters illuminated along the banks.
A specialized museum showcasing the history and culture of the Six Dynasties, featuring modern architectural design.
